programme.desc: After Mr. Burns hits Bart with his car, ambulance-chasing attorney Lionel Hutz persuades Homer and Marge to sue for a million dollars.

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Pike gets his head caught in the park railings and the platoon is summoned to help. Meanwhile a large bomb has exploded near the town and has put out the gas and water supplies. When Mainwaring returns to hear what has happened he puts the town under martial law.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross captures the beauty of Alaska on canvas: clear skies, soft cottony clouds, snow covered mountains and breathtaking crystal lake.

programme.title: My Little Pony: Friendship Is Magic
programme.desc: Twilight realizes she never returned a book to the Canterlot Library and may have caused her favorite librarian to lose her job.

programme.title: My Little Pony: Friendship Is Magic
programme.desc: Rainbow Dash's old pal Quibble Pants needs help to bond with the daughter of his new special friend because, like Dash, she's a real sports pony.

programme.title: The Fresh Prince of Bel-Air
programme.desc: When Will is having trouble with history he tries to pass the blame on the school for not including black history in their curriculum. Viv offers to teach and Will is hoping for an easy ride but instead Viv works him to the bone. And when she's about to leave, the entire class except Will asks her to stay on.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: One Fish, Two Fish, Blowfish, Blue Fish
programme.desc: After the family have visited the Happy Sumo sushi restaurant, Homer is led to believe that he has eaten a poisonous fugu fish and will be dead by the next day. He therefore makes a list of all the things he must do such as making his peace with his father and insulting Burns,which gets him sacked. Preparing to die, he falls asleep listening to a tape of Larry King reading the Bible but next morning wakes up to find he was not poisoned after all.

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: On a field exercise acting as commandos meeting a secret agent, represented by Mainwaring, the platoon must trust no-one for GHQ has sent out counter agents to catch them. The appearance of a gorilla compounds their problem.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers
programme.desc: It's foul play when the guys work stadium security! Then, the guys contaminate evidence during a crime scene investigation class. Plus, tonight's loser develops multiple personality disorder when opening for Legends in Concert.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin are wary of Anais' new friend, Josh, making her question his motives as well.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is property shopping with a couple of nature lovers who yearn for great views and open spaces, and they're willing to move 500 miles to the Scottish Highlands to find them. With a budget of £325,000, can Jules find them their dream home? Away from the house hunt, Jules samples a Scottish brew of a rather different kind.

programme.title: Clarkson's Farm
programme.desc: As Covid-19 puts Britain on lockdown, Jeremy suddenly finds that his biggest worries aren't all related to the upcoming lambing season.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares
programme.desc:
What's the BEST RECIPE for a successful restaurant? Top French chefs, the finest ingredients, food fit for a king...
Fine dining restaurant La Riviera in Inverness has it all and more.
All except one teeny weeny thing... CUSTOMERS. Without them, you don't stand a CHANCE.
And La Riviera is running out of TIME and MONEY.
La Riviera is on a MISSION: to bring sophisticated French cuisine to the home of haggis, tatties and deep-fried Mars bars. So it's the BEST of everything. It boasts a real DREAM COOKING TEAM: top French chef Loic Lefebvre who's worked in some of the best restaurants in the world and an impeccably-trained kitchen staff with Michelin star-studded backgrounds. The produce is ultra-fresh and top quality - the veg is flown in from France. Even the equipment is top of the league. So far, so good.
But delivering the very best costs... BIG TIME. Multi-millionaire owner Barry Larson is tearing his hair out as La Riviera is costing him a cool £8,000 a week to run - and that's on t

programme.title: Lucky Star
programme.desc: Everything is normal as the four main characters attend a normal day of high school. They discuss different foods and how they eat them. Later on, Kagami gets sick and all the characters go over to make sure she is okay.

programme.title: Fate/Apocrypha
programme.desc: Believing a Servant could be behind the string of gruesome murders terrorizing Romania, the Mage's Association sends in Kairi to investigate.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin search for adventure during a walk around Elmore.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Stephen Fry considers some hypothetical questions with panellists Sandi Toksvig, John Lloyd, Johnny Vegas and Alan Davies.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.sub-title: Mel Giedroyc, Chris Tarrant, Alexander Armstrong, Alex Jones
programme.desc: Rob Brydon is back in the host's chair for the sixth series of this comedy panel show. Lee Mack is joined by Alex Jones and Alexander Armstrong and David Mitchell is joined by Chris Tarrant and Mel Giedroyc.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: It's Valentine's Day, and Cuddy has a blind date. Meanwhile, House leaves ER duty to take the case of a child patient named Hannah.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: After finding a secret manifesto on Alan's memory stick, Gumball and Darwin decide to foil his plan for world domination.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard goes to the Nordschleife to see if Sabine Schmitz can do a lap of the infamous track in a Transit van in under 10 minutes. Jeremy races a Fiat Panda against a marathon runner around the London Marathon circuit during the Tuesday morning rush-hour and the TVR Sagaris is featured. Justin Hawkins is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.

programme.title: An Idiot Abroad
programme.desc: Karl Pilkington finally returns home after travelling around the world to see the Seven Wonders of the World. Back in Britain he's quizzed about his travels abroad by his friends Ricky Gervais and Stephen Merchant, who want to find out if he's actually learned anything from his experiences.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Nicole reminisces about how she met Richard during a disastrous family dinner, and how things could have turned out for her if she had not.

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005)
programme.desc: The TARDIS takes the Doctor and Martha to New Earth in the year Five Billion and Forty-Three, where the Face of Boe finally reveals his message to the Time Lord; meanwhile, terrifying beasts are lurking beneath the city of New New York - creatures that the Doctor believed he defeated many, many years ago.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc:
Chandler plans to propose to Monica at dinner, but his plans go awry when Richard shows up at the same restaurant. Meanwhile, Phoebe and Joey join Rachel at a charity auction only to discover that Joey isn't aware what a silent auction is. And Ross and Elizabeth's relationship comes to a crossroad.
Chandler continues to pretend to hate the idea of marriage, unaware that Richard wants Monica back. Meanwhile, Rachel and Phoebe discuss their 'back-up' plans, if they are not married by the time they're 40.

programme.title: Fate/stay night
programme.desc: Shinji says things like how he has no intention of fighting and he was just dragged into this war. He asks to make an alliance with Shirou to go after Rin but Shirou refuses. Shinji says because he doesn't know magic other Masters can't detect him so that means Shinji isn't the other Master Rin was detecting, meaning there is a fourth one at the school. Before Shirou leaves Shinji tells Shirou about how one of the Masters has set up base at a temple.Shirou gets home and is yelled at by Saber and Rin, Rin having come over after Shirou didn't come home. Shirou tells them about Shinji and the Master at the temple.Shirou and Rin both agree, against Sabers judgment, that they should not attack the temple yet as they don't know what Servant and it may be a trap.Saber brings up again with Shirou the temple but Shirou insists that they won't be going.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Taffy and Claire find an old lady on the street with a bird cage and a suitcase. She has run away from a home and later dies in the canteen. Tosh and Jim stop and search a cat burglar who uses his socks to avoid leaving fingerprints. Dashwood and Viv try to catch, unsuccessfully, 2 pickpockets until Sgt. Peters gives them a hint.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Jim has brought a new suit. 27 motorbikes including a police bike have been nicked. Garfield and Viv notice 2 youths stealing a bike. When they raid the garage all they find is the license plate of the police bike. Able is assigned to deliver a notification of death. A case that Melvin spent 2 weeks on is going to be dropped before going to court. The man involved is going to complain about Melvin who nicks him for assaulting a police officer.

programme.title: The Transformers
programme.desc: Both Autobots and Decepticons are facing tough times as they are in need of the element called Cybertonium. The Decepticons quickly get as much as they need, but the only way for the Autobots to get it is to rely on The Dinobots, who have been unaffected by all this. However, the Dinobots have been getting annoyed with The Autobots lately and leave them.

programme.title: The Transformers
programme.desc: Both Autobots and Decepticons are facing tough times as they are in need of the element called Cybertonium. The Decepticons quickly get as much as they need, but the only way for the Autobots to get it is to rely on the Dinobots, who have been unaffected by all this. However, the Dinobots have been getting annoyed with the Autobots lately and leave them.

programme.title: Fate/stay night
programme.desc: Shinji says things like how he has no intention of fighting and he was just dragged into this war. He asks to make an alliance with Shirou to go after Rin but Shirou refuses. Shinji says because he doesn't know magic other Masters can't detect him so that means Shinji isn't the other Master Rin was detecting, meaning there is a fourth one at the school. programme.title: Fate/Apocrypha
programme.desc: Saber of Black vanishes after giving his heart to the young homunculus boy. Ruler swears to do everything in her power to grant him his freedom.

programme.title: The Fresh Prince of Bel-Air
programme.desc: Granny comes for a visit and the family insists that she not do anything except Will who thinks she's cool. Yet, her country ways don't jive to just sitting around so she runs herself ragged cleaning up the house. Uncle Phil tells Will to leave her alone, but Granny insists that she and Will attend the Heavy D concert so they engineer a plan to sneak out. When the ruse is uncovered, she and Phillip have a talk about letting her live her own life.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: The gang celebrate Monica and Chandler's engagement. Phoebe wants to be the musician at the wedding and comes up with a new song, while Joey tries to dress and act like a 19-year-old for an audition. Ross and Rachel briefly reunite and after Monica catches them kissing, she's convinced that Rachel is trying to steal her thunder.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge and Homer tell the children how they first met - as high school students in 1974. Homer tried to impress Marge by pretending to be a French student to take her to the prom but she chose Artie Zif as her escort instead. However after Artie has made a nuisance of himself Marge rejects him and returns to the man she will marry.

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: The platoon dresses up as Morris Dancers as part of a carnival to raise money for the town's Spitfire fund, which is still £2,000 short. A Lady Godiva figure will lead the parade but there is confusion over who this will be.

programme.title: The Old Grey Whistle Test
programme.sub-title: Roberta Flack / Mike Absalom / The Rolling Stones / Frank Zappa
programme.desc:
Roberta Flack: Sonny and Sister Joy / I Never Dreamed You'd Leave in Summer
Mike Absalom: Hector the Dope Sniffing Hound / Saga of WPC Sadie Stick
The Rolling Stones: Happy, All Down The Line (concert clips)
Frank Zappa: Cleetus Awreetus Awrightus (filmfinders clip)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross creates a tiny waterfall tucked beside a glassy lake, while surrounded by snowy majestic peaks and distant evergreens.

programme.title: My Little Pony: Friendship Is Magic
programme.desc: When Sandbar asks Yona to a pony dance, she goes to Rarity and her friends for a makeover in appearance and personality.

programme.title: My Little Pony: Friendship Is Magic
programme.desc: Grogar sends his legion of doom on a mission to become allies, but his plan works too well and they almost become friends.

programme.title: The Fresh Prince of Bel-Air
programme.desc: Jazz asks Will the favor of taking his sister out on a date after she moves into town, Will turns it down until Jazz introduces the two. Will takes her on a date and learns that she has her whole life planned out for the two already.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: Homer vs. Lisa and the 8th Commandment
programme.desc: Lisa is shocked when Homer does an under the counter deal with a television installation engineer and gets cable T.V. for free as it is against the eighth commandment - Thou Shalt Not Steal. Lisa and Homer both,unsuccessfully, try to convince each other of their point of view but,after consultation with the Reverend Lovejoy,Lisa knows exactly what she must do - pull the plug whilst everybody is watching the big fight at on Homer's television.

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Lady Maltby offers her Rolls Royce to Capt. Mainwaring for use as his staff car. After the car has been disguised with camouflage paint, it has to be repainted black, because the mayor's car has run out of petrol, and there's no official car to greet a visiting VIP.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers
programme.desc: Joe, Sal, Q, and Murr ham it up behind the counter of a famous NYC deli, and head to Union Square where they break the ice with bizarre behavior while selling popsicles. And tonight's loser wishes Bingo wasn't his name-o in a humiliating and cringe-worthy punishment.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: It turns out the Wattersons have been using the Robinsons' WiFi, but when Mr. Robinson changes the password, the Watterson household is left without internet and Gumball and Darwin attempt to recreate it.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple of equestrian enthusiasts swap suburban life in Farnborough, Hampshire, for a new rural existence in Somerset. Their budget of £600,000 needs to secure not only a country home but also land for their horses. In the country, Nicki wades through mud as she encounters an age old fishing technique.

programme.title: Clarkson's Farm
programme.desc: After months of hard work Clarkson's farm arrives at a make or break moment; harvest time. Off to a rocky start, Clarkson must find a vital piece of equipment and fit four days of work into 24 hours, to harvest hundreds of acres of crops.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares
programme.desc: Saturday night at The Glass House Restaurant in Ambleside was a disaster. Orders in the kitchen were mixed up, the food wasn't cooked properly and the customers were complaining. Not only was the chef in tears, but the owners were on the brink of bankruptcy. A few months on, there have been some departures from the Glass House. Who is in charge of the kitchen now? And, more importantly, is Ramsay's Caesar salad still on the menu?

programme.title: Lucky Star
programme.desc: Throughout Golden Week, Tsukasa and Kagami have been playing together. However, Tsukasa does not know that her sister has actually been waking up earlier and going to bed later than her in order to study and finish homework. After the break, Konata does an all-nighter and manages to obtain an above-average score despite doing no studying during the short vacation. Later, Konata decides to get a job at a cosplay café in order to fund her otaku desires.

programme.title: Fate/Apocrypha
programme.desc: Believing a Servant could be behind the string of gruesome murders terrorizing Romania, the Mage's Association sends in Kairi to investigate.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: When he is labeled a "loser" in an online test, Gumball believes he must do the opposite of everything he would normally do. Meanwhile, Darwin and Sarah notice Tobias is stealing Gumball's life and role in the series in the form of a cheesy sitcom.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Stephen Fry asks unanswerable questions about house and home, with Bill Bailey, Eddie Izzard, Danny Baker and Alan Davies.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.sub-title: Richard Madeley, Sanjeev Bhaskar, Kate Humble, Miles Jupp
programme.desc: Team captain David Mitchell is joined by TV presenter Richard Madeley and actor Sanjeev Bhaskar, while his counterpart Lee Mack welcomes naturalist Kate Humble and comedian Miles Jupp. Host Rob Brydon oversees the comedy panel show as the contestants try to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: House encounters a brain-damaged musical prodigy with inexplicable abilities while the team faces serious concerns about House's own health.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Rocky decides to stop living life online, and find love on a dating app, and Gumball and Darwin try to help him.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The lads take some convertibles to Iceland. Specifically the Crossfire, 350Z, and the TT. The Ferrari F430 finally makes an appearance at the Top Gear test track, in Berlinetta and Spyder form. Sir Tim Rice is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.

programme.title: An Idiot Abroad
programme.desc: Karl is joined by Warwick Davis as they attempt to follow in the footsteps of Marco Polo. They begin their adventure in Venice.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin give Bobert instructions so he will behave in a safe and responsible manner, but he applies his logic to their rules.

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005)
programme.desc: The Doctor investigates disappearances in New York at the height of the depression in the 1930s. Nefarious Pig Men linger in the sewers and the Daleks prepare an audacious plan at the top of the Empire State Building

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: As Monica starts planning her wedding she's shocked to find out that her parents have spent her entire wedding fund. However, her spirits are restored when she discovers that Chandler has enough savings for her dream wedding scenario. Phoebe temporarily moves into Ross's apartment, bringing her massage clients with her. This leads to some weird encounters for Ross. Meanwhile, Joey mocks Rachel when he finds an erotic novel she is reading.

programme.title: Fate/stay night
programme.desc: Shinji says things like how he has no intention of fighting and he was just dragged into this war. He asks to make an alliance with Shirou to go after Rin but Shirou refuses. Shinji says because he doesn't know magic other Masters can't detect him so that means Shinji isn't the other Master Rin was detecting, meaning there is a fourth one at the school. programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: June and Melvin call at a school where a baby has been taken. June thinks she knows who took the baby. Viv and Able are called to a basement flat where the old lady has been conned by the 'maggot man'. He is a bogus water board worker who shows people maggots supposedly behind pipes. Tosh tries out a new car and then tells Jim about a car scam that is going on.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Frazer brings in a suspected shoplifter. Frazer is having her apprasial interview later. A woman comes in to inform Frazer that there might be problems at a talk given by a pro abortionist.

programme.title: The Transformers
programme.desc: From the moon, Megatron manages to interfere with the radio transmissions on Earth. Blaster and Cosmos go to put an end to his plot as the other Autobots work to help those affected by the loss of radio transmissions.

programme.title: The Transformers
programme.sub-title: A Decepticon Raider in King Arthur's Court
programme.desc: Spike, Hoist and Warpath find their battle with a group of Decepticons interrupted when they all get sent back in time to the middle ages.

programme.title: Fate/stay night
programme.desc: Shinji says things like how he has no intention of fighting and he was just dragged into this war. He asks to make an alliance with Shirou to go after Rin but Shirou refuses. Shinji says because he doesn't know magic other Masters can't detect him so that means Shinji isn't the other Master Rin was detecting, meaning there is a fourth one at the school. Before Shirou leaves Shinji tells Shirou about how one of the Masters has set up base at a temple.Shirou gets home and is yelled at by Saber and Rin, Rin having come over after Shirou didn't come home. Shirou tells them about Shinji and the Master at the temple.Shirou and Rin both agree, against Sabers judgment, that they should not attack the temple yet as they don't know what Servant and it may be a trap.Saber brings up again with Shirou the temple but Shirou insists that they won't be going.

programme.title: Fate/Apocrypha
programme.desc: Saber of Black vanishes after giving his heart to the young homunculus boy. Ruler swears to do everything in her power to grant him his freedom.

programme.title: The Fresh Prince of Bel-Air
programme.desc: Geoffrey's old employer Lord Fowler and his daughter come to the Banks' house for the weekend. Will wants to use the car to go to a Ziggy Marley concert in Nevada, Philip and Viv won't let him so he makes a proposition to be on his best behavior for the week. Lady Penelope winds up not being what Will thought she was.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica tries to figure out Phoebe's grandmother's secret chocolate chip cookie recipe. Meanwhile, Rachel tries to teach Joey how to sail, and Chandler has an awkward encounter when he tries to bond with Monica's father.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Selma is looking for a husband and asks Marge to help her find one; Marge in turn asks Homer to help. Homer thinks that Principal Skinner is a good prospect and plans Skinner for a setup with Selma. However, when Homer arranges for them to meet, Skinner falls for Patty instead, to everyone's chagrin.

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: After the platoon accidentally shoots a turkey, attempts are made to return it to the local farmer, but he will not accept it so the men come up with the idea of providing a dinner for the local OAPs.
